title: Verify Whether The Logged-In User Has Restricted Access For AGV Removal Actions
summary: Use this source-based check to confirm whether the current application session appears consistent with the training guidance that access is controlled by username and password, that not everybody has credentials, that operators do not have the credentials for this action, and that supervisors do. This is an access-verification procedure only and not an AGV removal procedure.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing whether a currently logged-in application session should have access to AGV removal-related controls or permissions, especially before any high-impact AGV action is attempted.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to perform AGV removal.
Do not use this runbook as authorization to click AGV removal controls.
Do not use this runbook when the user's role or expected permissions cannot be identified from available information.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the logged-in session, compare the observed user role or expected access level to the source-described restriction, and document whether access appears aligned.
Per source guidance, operators should not have credentials for this action.
Escalation target if an operator appears to have AGV removal access or if an unauthorized session is available for use.

safety_notes: The source describes removing an AGV from an active system as one of the worst actions a user can do.
Do not attempt AGV removal as part of this verification.
Treat visible AGV removal capability as high impact and verify user appropriateness before any action is attempted.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the application login/session state
Knowledge of the current user's role or expected permissions
Visibility into AGV-related controls if present

access_required: Ability to review the current application session or login state
Ability to identify the current user or expected role
system_preconditions: An application session is available for review
If AGV-related controls are being reviewed, they are visible in the current interface

visual_references.description: Training video frame associated with the warning about AGV removal on an active system and the statement that not all users have credentials; operators should not have access while supervisors may.

steps.title: Confirm the session is tied to a logged-in user account
steps.purpose: Establish that the application access being reviewed is associated with a username-and-password login as described in the source.
steps.instruction: Review the current application session and confirm that it is tied to a logged-in user account with a username and password, consistent with the source description.
steps.expected_result: The reviewer can confirm that the session is a credentialed login session rather than an unidentified or anonymous access state.
steps.healthy_condition: A logged-in user account is present and the session appears to be associated with credentialed access.
steps.failure_condition: The session cannot be tied to a logged-in user account, or the reviewer cannot determine whether the session is credentialed.

actions.label: Review login/session state
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the application session is tied to a logged-in user account with a username and password, as described in the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The session cannot be associated with a known logged-in user.
A logged-in session appears to be left available for unauthorized use.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The application has a username and password; not everybody has credentials. Operators don't have the credentials; supervisors do.

steps.title: Compare the current user's role to the stated access restriction
steps.purpose: Determine whether the current user's expected access level matches the source statement about who should and should not have credentials for this action.
steps.instruction: Identify the current user's role or expected access level and compare it to the source statement that operators should not have credentials for this action while supervisors do.
steps.expected_result: The reviewer can determine whether the user's role is consistent with the source-described restriction.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed user role aligns with the source guidance, such as an operator not having this level of access.
steps.failure_condition: The observed user role does not align with the source guidance, such as an operator appearing to have this level of access.

actions.label: Identify user role
actions.exact_action: Identify the current user's role or expected access level and compare it to the source statement that operators should not have credentials for this action while supervisors do.

stop_or_escalate_if: An operator appears to have access to AGV removal actions.
The user's role or expected permissions cannot be determined.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The application has a username and password; not everybody has credentials. Operators don't have the credentials; supervisors do.

steps.title: Verify visible AGV-related controls are being viewed by an appropriate user
steps.purpose: Prevent high-impact action from being attempted by a user who should not have that level of access.
steps.instruction: If reviewing a screen with AGV-related controls, verify whether the logged-in user is someone who is expected to have that level of access before any high-impact action is attempted.
steps.expected_result: The reviewer confirms whether the visible AGV-related controls are being accessed by a user whose role is consistent with the source restriction.
steps.healthy_condition: If AGV-related controls are visible, the logged-in user appears appropriate for that access level and no AGV removal is attempted.
steps.failure_condition: AGV-related controls are visible to a user who should not have that access, or there is pressure to attempt AGV removal during the check.

actions.label: Review AGV-related controls
actions.exact_action: If reviewing a screen with AGV-related controls, verify whether the logged-in user is someone who is expected to have that level of access before any high-impact action is attempted.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Application area where AGV-related controls are visible and the training context warning that AGV removal is high risk and access is credential-restricted.

stop_or_escalate_if: An operator appears to have access to AGV removal actions.
A logged-in session is left available for unauthorized use.
Anyone attempts or requests AGV removal as part of this verification.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Removing an AGV from an active system is described as the worst thing a user can do; the speaker says 'Don't remove an AGV.'
The application has a username and password; not everybody has credentials. Operators don't have the credentials; supervisors do.

steps.title: Record whether observed access aligns with the restriction
steps.purpose: Document the outcome of the access verification against the source-described restriction.
steps.instruction: Record whether the observed access aligns with the source-described restriction that not everybody has credentials.
steps.expected_result: A clear determination is documented stating whether the observed access is aligned or misaligned with the source guidance.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded result states that access is consistent with the source-described restriction.
steps.failure_condition: The recorded result shows access inconsistent with the source-described restriction or the reviewer cannot make a determination.

actions.label: Document access alignment
actions.exact_action: Record whether the observed access aligns with the source-described restriction that not everybody has credentials.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observed access does not align with the source-described restriction.
The reviewer cannot determine whether access is aligned.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The application has a username and password; not everybody has credentials. Operators don't have the credentials; supervisors do.

success_criteria: The current session is confirmed to be tied to a logged-in user account.
The current user's role or expected access level is compared against the source statement.
If AGV-related controls are visible, the reviewer confirms whether the logged-in user is appropriate for that access level.
The outcome is recorded as aligned or not aligned with the source-described restriction.
healthy_conditions: Credentialed access is present and attributable to a known user.
Operators do not appear to have AGV removal access.
Any visible AGV-related controls are being viewed by a user whose role is consistent with the source guidance.
No AGV removal is attempted during the verification.
failure_conditions: An operator appears to have access to AGV removal actions.
A logged-in session is left available for unauthorized use.
The reviewer cannot determine the current user's role or whether the session is credentialed.
The observed access does not align with the source-described restriction.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if an operator appears to have access to AGV removal actions.
Escalate if a logged-in session is left available for unauthorized use.
Stop the verification and escalate if anyone attempts to use this check to justify removing an AGV.
Escalate when the reviewer cannot determine whether the observed access is appropriate.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training frame associated with AGV-related interface context and the warning that AGV removal is high risk and restricted by credentials.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The application has a username and password; not everybody has credentials. Operators don't have the credentials; supervisors do.
Removing an AGV from an active system is described as the worst thing a user can do; the speaker says 'Don't remove an AGV.'

missing_details: The source does not provide a named application screen specifically dedicated to AGV removal access verification.
The source does not define a formal permission matrix beyond stating that operators do not have credentials and supervisors do.
The source does not specify the exact documentation location or system of record for recording the verification result.
The source does not identify a specific escalation owner by name or team.
